creative_brief: Hero image: extreme close-up of a marbled grass-fed steak on butcher paper, a handwritten farm-name tag visible (e.g. 'Rachel's Farm, Kempsey NSW'). Muted earth tones, natural light, no supermarket styling. Below the fold, a simple map graphic showing a short paddock-to-hub line versus a long 2,400km supermarket route. Clean, editorial layout — let the food and the farms do the talking.
headline: Your supermarket beef travelled 2,400km. Ours travelled a paddock.
subhead: 100% grass-fed, regenerative beef — days old, not weeks. From named farms you can actually visit. Collected fresh from your local hub.
hero_copy: Somewhere right now, a vacuum-sealed steak is sitting in a cold truck on day eleven of a 2,400-kilometre journey. No farm name on the label. No way to know what that animal ate, or how it lived. We thought grocery shopping deserved better than a guessing game. FarmThru sources 100% from regenerative farms — animals raised on pasture, no hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing. You order online. You collect from Brookvale, Monday to Friday. And every cut tells you exactly where it came from.

sections.heading: You can taste what care looks like
sections.body: Rachel Ward left acting to farm regeneratively in Kempsey, NSW. She rebuilt soil. She raised cattle on open pasture. She proved that doing right by the land produces food that tastes unmistakably different. Her beef is one of the products you will find at FarmThru — alongside wild-caught Alaskan salmon from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast, pork belly and fennel salami from Bundarra Farm, bio-dynamic dairy from Paris Creek Farms in Meadows SA, and eggs from Farmer Brown in NSW. These are not anonymous suppliers. They are named farmers doing named work. Supermarket beef can sit in cold storage for 14 to 21 days. Ours goes from farm to hub to your kitchen in days. That gap is not marketing. You will taste it in the first bite.

sections.heading: Why a hub, not a delivery van
sections.body: Home delivery sounds convenient until you pay for it — in surcharges, in emissions, in food sitting on a doorstep. Our hub-and-collect model strips out the cost layers that inflate your grocery bill. No warehouse rent on prime retail strips. No last-mile logistics. Just a clean, local hub where your order waits for you, kept exactly as it should be. No commitment. No subscription lock-in. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale, Monday to Friday. This is how we keep regenerative food priced for everyday families — not just the farmers' market crowd.

sections.heading: What if you could own a piece of it
sections.body: We are building something that does not exist yet — a grocery store where every product traces back to a regenerative farm, and every customer knows the farmer by name. Right now, you can be part of it. Join the waitlist for our equity crowdfunding campaign and be among the first to know when it opens. This is not a donation. It is a chance to own a piece of a food system that works differently. Place a fully refundable $5 deposit to secure your spot. No obligation. No pressure. Just a signal that you believe grocery shopping should mean something more than scanning barcodes under fluorescent lights.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is considering raising funds through equity crowdfunding. Any investment opportunity will be subject to disclosure requirements under Australian law. Investing in early-stage companies carries significant risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are projected or guaranteed. Any future offer will be made via a licensed intermediary platform and accompanied by an offer document. This content is not financial advice. Please consider your own financial situation and seek independent advice before making any investment decision.
